education.honors: James Scholar in the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences

research.name: FOCAL Lab
research.subtitle: Formal Methods of Computing \& Verifiable Constrained LLM Decoding
bullets: Researching verified generation of custom constrained decoding algorithms (CSDs) designed to effectively provide syntactic and semantic correctness.
Implementing Dafny-based contracts to ensure LLM-generated CSDs provably satisfy conditions necessary for valid generation (eventual termination, grammatical correctness).
Evaluating the framework's improvement of several HuggingFace models (Qwen, Mistral, Llama 3) on benchmarks like GSM-Symbolic and FOLIO against other attempted solutions (CRANE, DINGO).

experience.name: AbbVie
experience.subtitle: Business Technology Solutions Intern
bullets: Built a Streamlit UI enabling CRUD operations for JSON configuration files used by an internal Generative AI API and migrated 300+ prompt templates into a PostgreSQL database.
Designed a modular configuration system enabling dynamic pipeline updates without code changes, improving iteration speed for internal AI workflows.

projects.name: High-Performance Memory Allocator
projects.subtitle: Custom TLSF Allocator in C
bullets: Developed a custom memory allocator (malloc, calloc, realloc, free) in pure C, outperforming highly optimized glibc by 1\% and placing 3rd overall on the competitive class leaderboard.
Designed a two-level segregated fit (TLSF) allocator utilizing 128-bit bitmask free-list indexing, localized arena allocation, and a deferred-free cache to optimize repeated alloc/free patterns.
Engineered aggressive block-level optimizations, including compressed 32-bit pointers, boundary-tag coalescing with footer elision, and flag-encoded headers for O(1) neighbor status verification.

projects.name: Squishy.ai
projects.subtitle: Text-to-Physics Soft Robotics Simulation Platform
bullets: Built a full-stack platform that translates natural language into executable soft robotics physics simulations via PyElastica (Cosserat rod theory), winning the Keywords AI (YC W24) Hackathon.
Architected a high-performance FastAPI backend utilizing Numba JIT compilation, coupled with a headless Matplotlib and FFmpeg rendering pipeline for seamless GIF generation.
Developed an interactive React/Vite frontend featuring real-time simulation visualization and LLM-routed code generation through the Keywords AI gateway.

projects.name: Abstract Algebra, the Game
projects.subtitle: Interactive Lean4 Proof Game
bullets: Developing an interactive Lean4 proof game hosted on adam.math.hhu.de to teach abstract algebra via guided theorem proving (developed for the AI for Math Summer Fellowship).
Designed and shipped World 1 (Group World, 12 levels), implementing a notation-agnostic MyGroup typeclass to cover group axioms, identity uniqueness, and cancellation laws.
Completed World 2 (Reflection World) focusing on abelian groups, subgroups, and normal groups, while actively architecting Worlds 3--5 for advanced topics like homomorphisms and group actions.

projects.name: Asynchronous Audio Relay System
projects.subtitle: Concurrent Networking in C
bullets: Developed a multithreaded TCP server in C (Linux/Raspberry Pi) to serve as an asynchronous store-and-forward audio relay for multiple concurrent clients.
Architected a custom client--server protocol using POSIX sockets and pthreads to efficiently manage connection lifecycles and concurrent message delivery.
Engineered a lock-free ring buffer for concurrent audio ingestion, drastically reducing synchronization overhead and improving system latency.

projects.name: SIGEcom
projects.subtitle: Trend-Based Algorithmic Trading \& Game Theory
bullets: Designed, backtested, and deployed multi-asset algorithmic trading strategies in Python, utilizing SMA, EMA+RSI, MACD, and statistical arbitrage approaches.
Integrated custom strategies into a live simulation framework to seamlessly trade real-time cryptocurrency and FX market data.
Achieved positive PnL across a diverse portfolio of assets, including BTC, ETH, SOL, INR, ZAR, and JPY.

projects.name: Financial Engineering
projects.subtitle: Options Theory \& Neural Network Applications
bullets: Implemented the Cox--Ross--Rubinstein binomial model in Python to price European and American options, verifying convergence toward Black--Scholes benchmark values.
Leveraged neural network de-Americanization techniques to convert market American option prices into equivalent European prices, enabling accurate implied volatility computation.
Analyzed early exercise boundaries and computational efficiency across various market regimes to optimize pricing accuracy.
